description: After the agent rephrased the question, candidate gives a substantive answer naming MuleSoft, platform events, payload validation, DataWeave, VPN connectivity. Should advance. From session 2115a63a turn 172.

judge_input.active_question_text: Using your primary iPaaS (MuleSoft, TIBCO, or Boomi), how would you implement a near-real-time order sync from Salesforce to an on-prem ERP? Walk through the flow end-to-end.
active_question_positive_evidence: Names specific platform components and connectors
Explains JSON/XML mapping with schema validation
Details reliability controls: retry with backoff, idempotency keys

active_question_rubric.excellent: Concrete iPaaS components, secure connectivity, reliability controls.

judge_input.active_question_evaluation_hint: Verify platform-specific depth and reliability controls.

active_question_signal_metadata.value: 5+ years hands-on production experience with at least one iPaaS/ESB platform (MuleSoft, TIBCO, or Dell Boomi)

active_question_remaining_probes.0: How would you handle retries and idempotency to avoid duplicate orders during transient failures?
active_question_remaining_probes.1: What connectivity approach would you use to reach on-prem?

5+ years hands-on production experience.signal_value: 5+ years hands-on production experience with at least one iPaaS/ESB platform (MuleSoft, TIBCO, or Dell Boomi)

recent_turns.text: Using your primary iPaaS, walk me through an end-to-end near-real-time Salesforce to on-prem ERP order sync.
Hi, I didn't quite understand the question. Can you please explain it to me?

OK, let me put it differently. Imagine a new Salesforce order comes in every few seconds and you need it in an on-prem ERP almost immediately; using your iPaaS, walk me through the end-to-end sync flow.

judge_input.candidate_utterance: Uh, sure. So like for a near real-time order sync, uh, I trigger from Salesforce using, uh, something like platform events or webhook notifications. Uh, MuleSoft would consume these events, validate payload structure, and then transform the Salesforce JSON into the ERP schema using DataWeave. Since ERP is on-prem, I'd establish secure connectivity via VPN. And for reliability I'd add retries with exponential backoff and idempotency keys on order IDs to avoid duplicates.
